title: Bumble Inc (BMBL) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Revenue Performance Amid Monetization Challenges
text: Bumble delivered strong Q4 2024 results with total revenue of $262 million, hitting the high end of guidance. The Bumble App generated $212 million in revenue, exceeding expectations despite a 4% year-over-year decline. The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $73 million with 28% margins and produced $114 million in free cash flow for the full year. The platform grew total paying users 5% to 4.2 million, though average revenue per paying user (ARPPU) declined 9% to $20.58, signaling monetization challenges. Management expects sequential paying user declines in Q1 2025 due to ongoing ecosystem health initiatives, indicating intentional user base adjustments. For Q1 2025, the company projects revenue between $242-$248 million (down 7-10%) and adjusted EBITDA of $60-$63 million with 25% margins. The company is sunsetting Fruitz and Official apps to focus resources, while investing in new features like the Discover tab to enhance engagement. Founder Whitney Wolfe Herd returned to an operating role as Executive Chair, emphasizing her commitment to restoring confidence and joy to the dating experience through improved matching and safety features.
